title: Handbook of Cognitive-Behavioral Therapies, Fourth Edition
book: Handbook of Cognitive-Behavioral Therapies, Fourth Edition

node_types.description: A specific cognitive-behavioral therapy model or approach (e.g., Cognitive Therapy, REBT, DBT, Schema Therapy, Mindfulness-Based Therapy, etc.)

properties.name: delivery_modes
properties.description: Formats in which the therapy is delivered (individual, group, digital, etc.)

properties.name: target_populations
properties.description: Populations for which the approach is designed or adapted

properties.name: cultural_adaptations
properties.description: Adaptations for cultural or religious contexts, including Islamic alignment if present

supported_by_chapters: PART I HISTORICAL, PHILOSOPHICAL, AND SCIENTIFIC FOUNDATIONS
1 Historical and Philosophical Bases of the Cognitive-Behavioral Therapies
2 Empirical Validation and the Cognitive-Behavioral Therapies
3 Cognitive Science Foundations of Cognitive-Behavioral Therapy
11 Mindfulness and Acceptance Interventions in Cognitive-Behavioral Therapy
12 Dialectical Behavior Therapy - A Comprehensive Cognitive-Behavioral Treatment for Borderline Personality Disorder, Emotion Dysregulation, and Difficult-to-Treat Behaviors

node_types.description: A method, tool, or process for evaluating client symptoms, functioning, or treatment progress

properties.name: name
properties.description: Name of the assessment tool or method

properties.name: assessment_type
properties.description: Type of assessment (e.g., self-report, interview, behavioral observation, physiological)

properties.name: psychometric_properties
properties.description: Reliability, validity, and other measurement properties

properties.name: cultural_considerations
properties.description: Notes on cultural adaptation or appropriateness

properties.name: application_context
properties.description: When and how the assessment is used (diagnosis, progress monitoring, outcome evaluation)

node_types.description: A structured, individualized conceptualization of a client's presenting problems, mechanisms, and treatment plan

properties.name: problem_list
properties.description: List of presenting problems across domains

properties.name: mechanisms
properties.description: Psychological mechanisms (e.g., dysfunctional beliefs, emotion regulation problems)

node_types.description: A representation of an individual client, including demographics, clinical characteristics, and cultural context
supported_by_chapters: PART IV APPLICATIONS TO SPECIFIC POPULATIONS
16 Cognitive-Behavioral Therapy for Comorbidity and Transdiagnostic Populations
18 Affirmative Cognitive-Behavioral Therapy with Culturally Diverse Populations

node_types.description: A structured plan outlining therapeutic goals, selected interventions, and monitoring strategies for a client
supported_by_chapters: 16 Cognitive-Behavioral Therapy for Comorbidity and Transdiagnostic Populations
18 Affirmative Cognitive-Behavioral Therapy with Culturally Diverse Populations

node_types.description: A defined group of clients sharing demographic, diagnostic, or contextual characteristics relevant to treatment adaptation

properties.name: group_type
properties.description: Type of group (e.g., youth, couples, comorbid, culturally diverse, prevention)

properties.name: treatment_adaptations
properties.description: Adaptations or considerations for this group

properties.name: common_risks
properties.description: Risks or challenges specific to the group

supported_by_chapters: PART IV APPLICATIONS TO SPECIFIC POPULATIONS
16 Cognitive-Behavioral Therapy for Comorbidity and Transdiagnostic Populations
18 Affirmative Cognitive-Behavioral Therapy with Culturally Diverse Populations

node_types.description: A representation of a therapist, including training, competencies, and cultural alignment

properties.name: competencies
properties.description: Therapist's clinical skills and areas of expertise

properties.name: cultural_competence
properties.description: Level and type of cultural competence

properties.name: supervision_experience
properties.description: Experience with supervision and ongoing training

properties.name: personal_alignment
properties.description: Therapist's alignment with client values (e.g., religious, cultural)

supported_by_chapters: 19 Going Beyond Didactic Training - How to Increase Utilization of Cognitive-Behavioral Therapy
18 Affirmative Cognitive-Behavioral Therapy with Culturally Diverse Populations

node_types.description: A specific therapeutic technique, exercise, or skill used within CBT approaches

properties.name: delivery_format
properties.description: How the technique is delivered (individual, group, digital, etc.)

properties.name: cultural_adaptations
properties.description: Adaptations for cultural or religious contexts

supported_by_chapters: 11 Mindfulness and Acceptance Interventions in Cognitive-Behavioral Therapy
12 Dialectical Behavior Therapy - A Comprehensive Cognitive-Behavioral Treatment for Borderline Personality Disorder, Emotion Dysregulation, and Difficult-to-Treat Behaviors

edge_types.description: Indicates that a therapy approach employs a specific intervention technique
supported_by_chapters: 11 Mindfulness and Acceptance Interventions in Cognitive-Behavioral Therapy
12 Dialectical Behavior Therapy - A Comprehensive Cognitive-Behavioral Treatment for Borderline Personality Disorder, Emotion Dysregulation, and Difficult-to-Treat Behaviors

edge_types.description: Indicates that a therapy approach has empirical support for use with a specific population group

properties.name: effect_size
properties.description: Summary of effect size or efficacy

properties.name: study_type
properties.description: Type of supporting studies (RCT, meta-analysis, etc.)

supported_by_chapters: 2 Empirical Validation and the Cognitive-Behavioral Therapies
16 Cognitive-Behavioral Therapy for Comorbidity and Transdiagnostic Populations

edge_types.description: Links a client profile to their individualized case formulation
Indicates that a case formulation is used to develop a treatment plan
Indicates that a treatment plan is designed to address specific problems or diagnoses in a client profile
supported_by_chapters: 16 Cognitive-Behavioral Therapy for Comorbidity and Transdiagnostic Populations

edge_types.description: Indicates that a treatment plan includes specific intervention techniques

properties.name: sequence
properties.description: Order or phase in which the technique is applied

supported_by_chapters: 11 Mindfulness and Acceptance Interventions in Cognitive-Behavioral Therapy
12 Dialectical Behavior Therapy - A Comprehensive Cognitive-Behavioral Treatment for Borderline Personality Disorder, Emotion Dysregulation, and Difficult-to-Treat Behaviors

edge_types.description: Indicates that a clinical assessment is used to evaluate a client profile

properties.name: assessment_timepoint
properties.description: When the assessment is applied (intake, progress, outcome)

edge_types.description: Indicates that a therapist is responsible for delivering a treatment plan

properties.name: delivery_mode
properties.description: Mode of delivery (in-person, telehealth, group, etc.)

supported_by_chapters: 19 Going Beyond Didactic Training - How to Increase Utilization of Cognitive-Behavioral Therapy
18 Affirmative Cognitive-Behavioral Therapy with Culturally Diverse Populations

edge_types.description: Indicates the degree of cultural, religious, or value alignment between therapist and client

properties.name: alignment_type
properties.description: Type of alignment (e.g., religious, linguistic, cultural)

properties.name: alignment_level
properties.description: Degree of alignment (e.g., high, moderate, low)

supported_by_chapters: 18 Affirmative Cognitive-Behavioral Therapy with Culturally Diverse Populations
